input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML (ItemsPage.xaml)**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

        <CollectionView.Header>
            <RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
                         IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
                <!-- wrapping trick only if needed -->
            </RefreshView>
        </CollectionView.Header>

    </CollectionView>

</ContentPage>
```

The cleaner, standard approach — wrap the entire `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}"
                    SelectionChangedCommandParameter="{Binding SelectedItem}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate>
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="›"
                           VerticalOptions="Center" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>

</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel (ItemsViewModel.cs)**

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigation _navigation; // or Shell.Current

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<Item> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private Item? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    public ItemsViewModel(IApiService api)
    {
        _api = api;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // must reset or spinner stays
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(
            $"{nameof(DetailPage)}?id={SelectedItem.Id}");

        SelectedItem = null;        // clear so user can re-select
    }
}
```

**Key binding points:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Drives the list |
| `SelectedItem` (TwoWay) | `SelectedItem` | Tracks selection; set to `null` after nav to allow re-selection |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires navigation logic |
| `IsRefreshing` (on RefreshView) | `IsRefreshing` | Controls the spinner; **must be set to `false`** when done |
| `Command` (on RefreshView) | `RefreshCommand` | Triggers your API reload |

**Things to watch out for:**
- **`IsRefreshing = false`** — if you forget this, the pull-to-refresh spinner never dismisses.
- **`SelectedItem = null`** — without this, tapping the same item twice won't fire `SelectionChanged` again.
- **`Mode=TwoWay`** on `SelectedItem` is required so the `null` reset propagates back to the view.
- Use `[QueryProperty]` on `DetailPage`'s VM to receive the `id` parameter from Shell navigation.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking for a .NET MAUI CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh, plus navigation to a detail page. This is a general knowledge question about MAUI/Xamarin XAML patterns — no codebase to explore. I'll provide the XAML and ViewModel explanation directly.
